abstract:
This research explores the implementation of text-rich environments within early childhood education (PAUD) units and examines their influence on functional language development. Adopting a qualitative case study approach at PAUD Shafa Marwah, data were gathered through systematic observations, in-depth interviews with educators and parents, and documentary analysis of lesson plans. The findings reveal that the successful integration of a text-rich environment—characterized by strategic classroom labeling, comfortable reading corners, and integrated play-based literacy activities—significantly engages young learners. Children demonstrated a heightened interest in reading materials and participated in more frequent, active interactions with various text forms. Consequently, this immersion led to measurable improvements in language acquisition, specifically regarding vocabulary expansion, reading comprehension, and oral communication skills. The study highlights the vital role of a stimulatory physical environment in fostering early literacy and expressive confidence in young children.

**Keywords:** Text-rich environment; language development; early childhood education; literacy immersion
